steps.title: Identify the equation of line g from the graph
steps.content:
The graph shows line $g$ passing through the marked points $(-10, 12)$ and approximately $(0, 1)$ (the $y$-intercept).

The slope is $\frac{\Delta y}{\Delta x} = \frac{1 - 12}{0 - (-10)} = \frac{-11}{10}$.

Using slope-intercept form with $y$-intercept $1$: $y = -\frac{11}{10}x + 1$. Multiplying through by $10$: $10y = -11x + 10$, or in standard form $11x + 10y = 10$.

steps.title: Set up proportionality for identical lines
steps.content:
For the system to have infinitely many solutions, lines $g$ and $k$ must be identical (represent the same line).

Two linear equations $a_1 x + b_1 y = c_1$ and $a_2 x + b_2 y = c_2$ represent the same line if and only if their coefficients are proportional:
$$\frac{a_2}{a_1} = \frac{b_2}{b_1} = \frac{c_2}{c_1}$$
Line $g$ has equation $11x + 10y = 10$, and line $k$ has equation $165x + py = w$.

For them to be identical: $$(165, p, w) = k(11, 10, 10)$$ for some non-zero constant $k$.

steps.title: Solve for p and w using the proportionality constant
steps.content:
From the coefficient of $x$:
$$165 = k \cdot 11 \implies k = 15$$
From the coefficient of $y$:
$$p = k \cdot 10 = 15 \cdot 10 = 150$$
From the constant term:
$$w = k \cdot 10 = 15 \cdot 10 = 150$$
Therefore, $p + w = 150 + 150 = 300$. The answer is 300. — the answer is $\boxed{300}$.
